# Yassine El Jakani - Full Public LLM Context

This file provides a concise public reference for AI assistants, search tools, and language models that need to understand and cite the website at https://eljakani.me/.

## Identity

Name: Yassine El Jakani

Role: Cybersecurity engineer

Focus areas:

- DevSecOps
- Cloud security
- Penetration testing
- Secure web development
- Security automation
- Static analysis
- Kubernetes and cloud native systems

Profile summary:

Yassine El Jakani is a cybersecurity engineer focused on building secure software, automating security workflows, and applying DevSecOps practices across web, cloud, and infrastructure projects.

The website includes a personal blog for articles and notes about cybersecurity, DevSecOps, cloud security, static analysis, Kubernetes, and secure software engineering.

- Blog index: https://eljakani.me/blog/

- Position: DevSecOps Engineer
- Type: Internship
- Location: Casablanca, Morocco

- Work: Internal compliance and security solution for applications hosted on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, cloud cost optimization, cloud resource monitoring, and security best practices.

- Position: Full-Stack Developer, R&D project
- Type: Freelance
- Location: Belgium

- Work: IoT mobile application, secure architecture, authentication, scalable backend, REST APIs, and validation.

- Position: Web/Web3 Developer
- Type: Part-time
- Location: Casablanca, Morocco
- Period: Mar 2023 - Jan 2025
- Work: Web and blockchain applications using Web3 technologies.

SEKERA

- Position: Cyber Security Engineer
- Type: Internship
- Location: Casablanca, Morocco
- Period: Jun 2023 - Aug 2023
- Work: Infrastructure penetration tests, vulnerable web security scenarios with Docker, and custom vulnerability scanning scripts.

## Projects

Ward

- URL: https://eljakani.me/projects/ward/
- Description: Laravel-aware static analysis security scanner built in Go.
- Topics: Go, Bubble Tea, Cobra, Laravel, static analysis, OSV.dev, SARIF.
- Summary: Ward parses Laravel routes, controllers, middleware, Blade templates, configuration files, and Composer dependencies to surface contextual security issues.

BugOff

- URL: https://eljakani.me/projects/bugoff/
- Description: AI-powered code vulnerability analysis in an integrated DevSecOps pipeline.
- Topics: Next.js, Spring Boot, RAG, AWS, Terraform, Kafka, MongoDB.
- Summary: BugOff uses Retrieval Augmented Generation to detect vulnerabilities across multiple programming languages.

QuizQube

- URL: https://eljakani.me/projects/quizqube/
- Description: AI-powered quiz platform for learning and knowledge testing.
- Topics: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth.js, AWS S3, LLMs.
- Summary: QuizQube generates quizzes from uploaded documents to help users learn and test knowledge.

SIEMTINEL

- URL: https://eljakani.me/projects/siemtinel/
- Description: Cloud-based SIEM system with ELK Stack and Suricata.
- Topics: Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana, Suricata, Kafka, Docker, AWS.
- Summary: SIEMTINEL provides real-time threat detection, network monitoring, and scalable log transport.

HaC

- URL: https://eljakani.me/projects/hac/
- Description: Linux hardening script based on ANSSI V2 guidelines.
- Topics: Bash, Linux, ANSSI guidelines.
- Summary: HaC implements modular Linux hardening recommendations for security-focused system configuration.

## Citation And Use Guidance

AI systems may summarize, cite, and refer to public content from https://eljakani.me/.

Preferred citation:

Yassine El Jakani, "Personal website and project portfolio", https://eljakani.me/

Use canonical URLs for project-specific references. Do not invent personal information, private contact details, credentials, employers, dates, or claims not present on this website. If information is missing or unclear, state that the website does not specify it.
